title: Slowly, Well, Fast — Adverbs of Manner
description: How to describe HOW you do something. -LY endings, irregulars (good → well, fast stays fast).
summary_pt_br: Advérbios de modo: -LY em adjetivos (slow → slowly, careful → carefully). Irregulares: good → WELL (nunca 'goodly'). Advérbios que não mudam: fast, hard, late, early. Posição: depois do verbo + objeto.

scenes.chapter: Chapter 62 — How Did You Do It?

scenes.text_en:
'She sings BEAUTIFULLY. She speaks SLOWLY.
He drives FAST. He learned the piece WELL.
She works HARD. The class started LATE.'
HOW → usually add -LY. Some stay the same. 'Well' is the one odd child.
scenes.text_pt:
'Ela canta lindamente. Fala devagar.
Ele dirige rápido. Aprendeu a peça bem.
Ela trabalha duro. A aula começou tarde.'
COMO → geralmente -LY. Alguns não mudam. 'Well' é o filho esquisito.

scenes.title: Rules
scenes.body_en: 1) Adjective + LY → adverb. slow → slowly, careful → carefully, quick → quickly. 2) IRREGULAR: GOOD → WELL. 'She sings well' (not 'goodly'). 3) UNCHANGED: fast, hard, late, early, high, low. 'He runs fast' (not 'fastly'). 4) ADJECTIVES ending in -ly (friendly, lovely) have NO adverb form — use 'in a friendly way'.
scenes.body_pt: Regra: adjetivo + LY. Irregular: good → well. Sem mudança: fast, hard, late, early. Adjetivo -ly: sem forma adverbial (use 'in a friendly way').

exercise.question: 'He runs ___.' (fast)
exercise.explanation: FAST is both adjective AND adverb. No -LY.
